purpose: To eliminate the manual translation of approved UI designs into frontend code by providing an autonomous, polyglot generation pipeline, significantly reducing time-to-market for new features and ensuring strict parity between UX intent and deployed code.

kpis.name: Visual Regression Failure Rate in CI

features.name: Figma Webhook Ingestion
features.description: Listen for component publish events directly from the external design tool to initiate the pipeline.

features.name: Agentic Code Generation
features.description: Utilize LLM execution harnesses to convert normalized design graphs into production-ready React ASTs.

features.name: Automated GitHub PR Creation
features.description: Automatically open pull requests with the generated code and tag the appropriate CODEOWNERS.

features.name: Component Atlas UI Dashboard
features.description: Provide a frontend interface for developers to preview, diff, and manually regenerate components.

userScenarios.scenario: Publishes a new 'Checkout Button' variant in the central Figma library.
userScenarios.expectedOutcome: The system automatically detects the change, parses the new styling, and queues it for code generation without the designer needing to manually alert engineering.

userScenarios.scenario: Needs to implement the new 'Checkout Button' in the web application.
userScenarios.expectedOutcome: The engineer finds an open Pull Request in GitHub containing the exact React component code for the button, reviews it, and merges it in minutes.

technical: Code generation must currently target React (styled-components) and backend endpoints in Fastify/FastAPI only.
business: LLM API usage costs must not exceed a max budget of $150 USD per day during Phase 1.
design: Generated CSS properties must map cleanly to our existing enterprise design token system.
